whatThisIs: Build a printable discharge-instruction template that covers the structural sections every hospital discharge document should carry: the primary diagnosis in plain language, the medication list with dose / frequency / start-and-stop dates and the changes since admission, the follow-up appointments with provider name and target date window, the return-precautions section ("come back if you experience..."), the activity and dietary restrictions, the work or school note when relevant, and the 24-hour callback contact. Sophie Well fills the structure from short prompts and outputs a clean discharge sheet ready to print and review.
whenToUse: Use this when an institutional discharge template is unavailable or unwieldy, when building a teaching example for nursing or resident orientation, or when reconciling a complex discharge across two providers and you need a structured worksheet that does not lose a field in the handoff. The output is a reference template - institutional discharge protocols, condition-specific guidelines (CHF, MI, post-op, OB), and any required regulatory components (e.g., Medicare IM notice timing) govern the final form. A clean discharge instruction is one of the highest-leverage interventions against 30-day readmission, so the template earns its keep on coverage, not artistry.
